edges.evidence_quote: The Islamic Resistance Movement , abbreviated Hamas ... is a Sunni Islamist Palestinian nationalist political organisation with a military wing known as the al-Qassam Brigades.
edges.context: Al-Qassam Brigades is the armed wing of Hamas.

edges.evidence_quote: It emerged from his 1973 Mujama al-Islamiya Islamic charity affiliated with the Muslim Brotherhood.
edges.context: Hamas emerged from a charity affiliated with the Muslim Brotherhood.

edges.evidence_quote: Initially, Hamas was discreetly supported by Israel , as a counter-balance to the secular Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) to prevent the creation of an independent Palestinian state.
edges.context: Hamas was used by Israel as a counter-balance to the PLO.

edges.evidence_quote: In the 2007 Battle of Gaza , Hamas seized control of the Gaza Strip from rival Palestinian faction Fatah.
edges.context: Hamas seized Gaza from Fatah in a violent conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Per Hamas officials, he was elected due to his considerable popularity in the Arab and Islamic worlds following the 7 October attacks and his strong connections with Iran and the " Axis of Resistance ," an informal Iranian-led political and military coalition.
edges.context: Hamas has strong connections with the Axis of Resistance.

edges.evidence_quote: On 14 August 2009, Hamas fighters stormed the Mosque of extremist cleric Abdel-Latif Moussa. The cleric was protected by at least 100 fighters from Jund Ansar Allah... The resulting battle left at least 13 people dead, including Moussa and six Hamas fighters.
edges.context: Hamas attacked and fought Jund Ansar Allah in a battle.

edges.evidence_quote: Hamas used to be strongly allied with Iran and Syria. Iran gave Hamas an estimated $13–15 million in 2011 as well as access to long-range missiles.

edges.evidence_quote: Hamas used to be strongly allied with Iran and Syria. Hamas's political bureau was once located in the Syrian capital Damascus before the Syrian civil war.
